title: Mexico beat South Africa in the opening Group A match
description: Mexico opened the tournament with a 2-0 win over South Africa. The result gave the co-host a clean start and immediately put pressure on the rest of Group A.
whyItMatters: Opening wins create immediate table leverage and reduce pressure before the second group match.

title: Brazil draw Morocco in Group C
description: Brazil started Group C with a 1-1 draw against Morocco. The result prevented Brazil from taking early control of the group and elevated Morocco as a credible challenger.
whyItMatters: A favorite dropping points early increases group volatility and makes later fixtures more consequential.

title: Germany rout Curacao 7-1
description: Germany produced the strongest early statement of the tournament with a 7-1 win over Curacao. The result created a major goal-difference cushion and established Germany as one of the tournament's form teams.
whyItMatters: A large opening win changes both the table math and the perception of a team's ceiling.

title: Canada rout Qatar 6-0
description: Canada beat Qatar 6-0 in Group B. The result turned Canada into an early dark-horse candidate and made goal difference a central factor in the group race.
whyItMatters: A heavy win can convert a contender from outsider to credible knockout-stage threat.

title: Brazil beat Haiti 3-0 to recover momentum
description: Brazil responded to the Morocco draw with a 3-0 win over Haiti. The result restored some momentum and improved Brazil's goal difference before the decisive Scotland match.
whyItMatters: The win reduced the cost of Brazil's opening draw and kept them positioned for group control.

title: Argentina beat Algeria 3-0
description: Argentina began Group J with a controlled 3-0 win over Algeria. The result gave Argentina immediate control and reinforced their profile as one of the most stable title contenders.
whyItMatters: A clean opening win gives an elite team flexibility to manage risk and rotation later in the group.

title: Argentina beat Austria 2-0
description: Argentina followed their opening win with a 2-0 victory over Austria. With two wins and no goals conceded, Argentina became one of the tournament's clearest early control teams.
whyItMatters: Back-to-back clean wins shift a team from promising start to near-secure qualification path.

title: Portugal draw DR Congo in Group K
description: Portugal opened Group K with a 1-1 draw against DR Congo. The result created early pressure because Colombia won their opener and took control of the group race.
whyItMatters: A favorite losing two points early can turn the second match into a pressure fixture.

title: Colombia beat Uzbekistan 3-1
description: Colombia beat Uzbekistan 3-1 in Group K. Combined with Portugal's draw, the result put Colombia in the early lead and increased pressure on Portugal.
whyItMatters: A rival's win can be as important as a favorite's dropped points because it changes the table leverage immediately.

title: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0
description: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 in Group F. The result strengthened Japan's dark-horse profile and made the final group match against Sweden a key test of their ceiling.
whyItMatters: Dominant wins by non-favorites reshape public expectations and create knockout-stage caution for traditional powers.
